data.title: CDT Equity increases bitcoin holding
data.description:
• CDT Equity (NASDAQ:CDT) announced on Friday the acquisition of 9.25648743 Bitcoin (BTC) for an aggregate purchase price of $1,005,000, at an average acquisition price of $108,301.75 per BTC. 
  • Following this transaction, CDT’s total Bitcoin holdings now stand at 17.9090111 BTC.
  • “CDT remains committed to strategically deploying capital within its treasury reserve program,” said James Bligh, Chief Financial Officer of CDT. “Our Bitcoin acquisitions form part of a disciplined, opportunistic approach to capital management, designed to align with the growing institutional adoption of digital assets, capitalize on market corrections, and strengthen our overall financial resilience.”
  • CDT +2.72% premarket to $3.77.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Apple iPhone Air sold out in China 'within minutes' of launch: report
data.description:
• Apple's (NASDAQ:AAPL) iPhone Air sold out in China “within minutes” of the smartphone's launch in the country, the South China Morning Post reported.
  • Apple shares were little changed in premarket trading on Friday.
  • Cupertino, Calif.-based Apple did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.
  • Pre-orders for the device, which was announced last month, started in China earlier this week after the major Chinese telecom operators received regulatory approvals for eSIM services. 
  • At just 5.6 millimeters thick, the iPhone Air is Apple's thinnest iPhone ever. The 6.5-inch device has a 120 Hz refresh rate and is made from spacecraft titanium. It comes in four different colors: sky blue, light black, gold and white; and it also has Ceramic Shield for better scratch resistance and toughness.
  • The iPhone Air comes with Apple's A19 Pro silicon, which includes a 6-core CPU and a 5-core GPU. 
  • News of the iPhone Air's reception in China comes during Apple CEO Tim Cook's visit to China. Cook pledged to increase investment in China during his visit, and recently met with Chinese Minister of Industry and Information Technology Li Lecheng.

data.title: Oklo to form partnership, receive up to $2B investment from reactor developer newcleo
data.description:
Oklo (NYSE:OKLO) +1.8% pre-market after announcing a joint agreement with newcleo, with the European advanced nuclear reactor developer planning to invest as much as $2 billion to develop advanced fuel fabrication and manufacturing infrastructure in the U.S.

Sweden's advanced nuclear technology developer Blykalla also is considering co-investing in the same projects and procuring fuel-related services from them, Oklo (NYSE:OKLO) said.

"The investments aim to span multiple projects under U.S. oversight, foster transatlantic cooperation that enhances energy security, and focus on creating a robust and resilient fuel ecosystem," Oklo (OKLO) said.

"This agreement to implement newcleo's advanced fuel expertise into Oklo's powerhouses and invest $2 billion into American infrastructure and advanced fuel solutions is yet another win for President Donald J. Trump's American Energy Dominance Agenda," U.S. Secretary of the Interior Burgum said.

data.title: United States Antimony says mining operations initiated at Stibnite Hill in Montana
data.description:
United States Antimony Corporation (NYSE:UAMY) on Friday said that it has initiated exploration and bulk sampling operations on Stibnite Hill, which is located directly adjacent to the Thompson Falls, Montana, smelting operation that is currently under significant expansion.

Antimony ore has now been trucked in several loads off the mountain to a flotation mill in Montana for crushing and sampling before further review by the company's metallurgical chemist. 

"Once the necessary permits were obtained from the DEQ this month, we began our exploration efforts. Those have resulted in our first four loads of raw antimony ore for our existing operations to begin processing this year," Joe Bardswich, Executive Vice President and Chief Mining Engineer for USAC, said.

"While we fully expected our first actual product from the company to come from our Alaska operations, the delay in permit approvals in that state for approximately five months, allowed Montana to be first." 

United States Antimony Corporation (NYSE:UAMY) stock down 3.2% premarket.

data.title: AppFolio in spotlight as KeyBanc upgrades to Overweight
data.description:
• AppFolio (NASDAQ:APPF) was in the spotlight on Friday as KeyBanc Capital Markets upgraded the real estate software company to Overweight from Sector Weight.
  • Shares were little changed in premarket trading.
  • “This week we attended AppFolio's annual FUTURE user conference. Conversations with customers renew our confidence that the Company is seeing momentum in both net new and expansions on Plus and Max,” KeyBanc analyst Jason Celino wrote in a note to clients. “More importantly, we come away with a better understanding of the attractive and TAM-expansive opportunities in monetizing the resident experience. With renewed conviction in AppFolio's ARPU growth story, we are raising our 2026 growth estimates and upgrading APPF to OW (from SW). With shares currently trading at 7.2x 2026 EV/sales, we view the recent pullback as a buying opportunity.”
  • Despite the upgrade, Celino kept his $285 price target on the stock.

data.title: WillScot reduces borrowing costs and extends $3B credit facility to 2030
data.description:
• WillScot Holdings (NASDAQ:WSC) announced the amendment of its asset-based lending (ABL) credit facility, lowering borrowing costs and extending the maturity to October 16, 2030.
  • The amended ABL revolver reduces the facility size from $3.7B to $3B, cutting undrawn line fees, while the accordion feature expands from $750M to $1B.
  • Interest rate spreads over Term SOFR and Term CORRA were reduced from 160 bps to 137.5 bps, with potential step-downs to 112.5 bps based on performance metrics.
  • At current borrowing levels, the company expects to save ~$5M annually in interest expense, with potential for further reductions.
  • The amendment enhances liquidity, with $1.4B in borrowing capacity available as of the transaction close.

data.title: Sphere 3D secures $4M from warrant exercise, issues new warrants in private placement
data.description:
• Sphere 3D (NASDAQ:ANY) entered into a warrant inducement agreement with an institutional investor for the immediate exercise of 4.37M existing warrants at a reduced price of $0.94, generating ~$4M in gross proceeds.
  • The funds will be used for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • In exchange, the investor will receive 8.74M new unregistered warrants (October 2025 series) in a private placement.
  • The new warrants will also have a $0.94 exercise price, become exercisable after stockholder approval, and will be valid for five years from the approval date.
  • The transaction is expected to close on or about October 17, 2025.
  • ANY shares down 9.3% premarket on Friday.
